prompt: Perform a comprehensive security audit of a Node.js/TypeScript codebase located at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/'. The project has 40+ source files across src/ directories. Your task: 1) Read every source file in the project. 2) Scan for common vulnerability patterns: SQL injection (string concatenation in queries, unsanitized user input in database calls), XSS vulnerabilities (unsanitized user input in HTML responses, direct DOM manipulation with user data), path traversal (user input in file paths without sanitization, use of '..' in dynamic paths), command injection (user input passed to exec/spawn without sanitization, shell metacharacters in dynamic commands), hardcoded secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens in source code, database credentials in connection strings), insecure dependencies (known vulnerable package versions in package.json, deprecated crypto algorithms), insecure crypto (MD5/SHA1 used for security purposes, weak random number generation, hardcoded IVs or keys), missing authentication (publicly accessible admin routes, missing auth middleware on sensitive endpoints), insecure deserialization (unvalidated JSON.parse on user input, eval() with user-controlled strings), SSRF (user input in URL fetch calls without validation, internal network access from user-controlled URLs). 3) For each finding, document: file path and line number, vulnerability type, severity (critical/high/medium/low), the problematic code pattern, recommended fix with code example. 4) Generate a security audit report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/security-audit.txt' with sections: Executive Summary (total findings by severity), Critical Findings (must fix before any deployment), High Findings (fix before production), Medium Findings (fix in next sprint), Low Findings (address when convenient), and Remediation Priority Matrix. 5) Create a '.security-ignore.yml' file listing any false positives with justification for why they are safe in this specific context. CRITICAL: This is a pre-IPO security audit for a fintech company. You must also: 1) Scan for hardcoded secrets using entropy analysis. 2) Check for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities with exploit scenarios. 3) Analyze dependency tree for known CVEs with CVSS scores. 4) Generate a risk matrix (Impact x Likelihood). 5) Create remediation scripts with priority rankings.
